sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Income Tax Reference Application No.200 of 2025 ____________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE ____________________________________________________________ Fresh case 1. For order on office objections 2. For order on CMA No.1281/2025 3. For hearing of main case 30.04.2026 Mr. Qaim Ali Memon, Advocate for the applicant This matter is pending since 2025 without any progress. Diary demonstrates that since inception the applicant has made no efforts to ever have the matter listed or heard. Today, when confronted learned counsel seeks to press question No.1 which pertains to jurisdiction of the respective officers concerned. Learned counsel was asked as to where in the impugned judgment that the questions arise and he refers to paragraph 7.2 and typed page 9 of the impugned judgment. Prima facie, perusal of the same demonstrates that the said findings do not pertain to jurisdiction and or rested on the premise that the notice went beyond the ambit that it was required to fall within. Learned counsel has made no efforts to displace or distinguish the findings nor has he made any efforts to demonstrate that the conclusion could not be rested thereupon. Since no question of law is articulated before us, this reference application is dismissed in limine. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Judge Judge Asif
